meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS starter kit to Solve tutorial hell (Intermediate Idea List)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome tutorial hell. A Intermediate-level Idea List using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: SaaS Starter Kit to Solve Tutorial Hell
content.seo_description: Break free from tutorial hell. Master SaaS starter kits using Antigravity and Claude Code to ship client projects faster and scale your freelance business.
content.answer_block: Tutorial hell ends when you stop consuming and start standardizing. By building a robust SaaS starter kit, you create a repeatable foundation for authentication, database management, and UI logic. Using Antigravity to scaffold and Claude Code to implement, you transform your workflow from manual coding into a high-speed production engine.
content.intro:
The screen flickers with another 'Build a To-Do List' tutorial, and the sinking feeling returns. You’ve spent months chasing syntax, yet your client projects remain stagnant, buried under a mountain of boilerplate code and repetitive setup tasks. You aren't learning; you're just stalling, trapped in a loop of passive consumption while the real work waits.

It’s time to break the cycle. We are pivoting from student to architect. By engineering a proprietary SaaS starter kit, you reclaim your agency and your hours. This isn't just about writing code; it's about building a delivery system that allows you to ship production-ready SaaS applications in days, not weeks. Let's build your competitive edge.

steps.title: Define Your Core Blueprint
steps.description: Establish a unified stack (Next.js, Supabase, Tailwind) that serves as the foundation for every client project.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a Next.js 14 project with Tailwind CSS, TypeScript, and Supabase integration configured for multi-tenant SaaS architecture.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Initialize project structure with standard authentication and database schemas'

steps.title: Implement Modular Authentication
steps.description: Create a reusable authentication module that handles social logins, magic links, and session management effortlessly.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a clean, reusable Auth component set using Supabase Auth that supports email/password and Google OAuth providers.

steps.title: Build the Payment Gateway Wrapper
steps.description: Stripe integration is the biggest bottleneck in SaaS. Pre-build a webhook listener and checkout flow template.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a robust Stripe integration module including webhook handlers for subscription events and a client-side checkout session generator.

steps.title: Automate Database Migrations
steps.description: Use Claude Code to standardize how your database schemas evolve so you never have to manually manage SQL files again.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Draft a migration script for a standard user-to-subscription schema mapping with proper foreign key constraints.

steps.title: Standardize UI Component Library
steps.description: Build a set of 'Copy-Paste' components tailored for your specific design language to ensure visual consistency.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a set of Shadcn-compatible UI components for common SaaS layouts: Sidebar, Data Table, and Pricing Cards.

pro_tips: Never build from scratch: Maintain a 'Golden Repo' that you clone for every new client project to ensure 80% of the work is already done.
Abstract your API calls: Use a central data-fetching layer so you can swap backends without rewriting your frontend components.
Prioritize Webhook robustness: Always build a local retry mechanism for Stripe webhooks; it saves hours of debugging production failures.
Version control your prompts: Keep a directory of high-performing Claude prompts in your repository to maintain consistency across AI sessions.
Focus on documentation: Write self-documenting code in your starter kit so that you can hand off projects to client teams without long training sessions.
content.next_steps: Refactor your existing client projects to use your new starter kit, then begin documenting your own custom 'Antigravity' modules for your most frequent use cases.
content.conversion_block: Stop chasing tutorials and start owning your output. By mastering your own modular starter kit, you become the developer clients pay for speed and reliability. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
